submissions.body:
My Biggest Struggle (Besides Surgery)

 

This past week i started annie cannons, and while recovering from surgery has definitely been hard, that actually wasn't my biggest struggle.

My biggest struggle was getting out of my own head.

Walking into something new-especially tech and coding- can feel intimidating. There's that little voicethat whispers,"Am I smart enough for this ? Can I really do this ?"When you come from a past like mine, where survival mode was the focus for so long, stepping into growth can feel unfamiliar ... even scary.

I realized im not just learning new skills- I'm learning a new version of myself. One who doesnt just survive, but builds. One who belongs in spaces I never imagined  id be in.

Balancing being a mom, healing physically, and stretching my brain in new ways is a lot. But this week showed me something important : the struggle isnt a stop sign- its proof im growing.

And growth is uncomfortable ...but its also powerful.

assignments.name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body:
🌸 jr. developer
i like this role because i like computers and puzzles. sometimes i get really focused on figuring things out and it makes me happy when the code finally works. i know i still have a lot to learn, but i think that’s okay because i love learning step by step. i get shy around people, so i like a job where i can just work on the computer and still be helpful.
i want to make apps or programs that help people and make life easier. i think i would be good at this because i don’t give up, even if i need extra time. i would bring patience, kindness, and creativity to the team.

🌸 content creator/editor
i like this role because i love writing little stories and sharing ideas. sometimes it is hard for me to talk to people face to face, but writing feels safe because i can take my time. i also like to make things sound nice and clear.
i think i could do good work with blogs, social media, or other writing because i pay attention to details. i don’t always spell everything perfect the first time, but i keep trying and i like to edit until it looks right. i want to make content that makes people smile or learn something new.

🌸 junior graphic designer
i like this role because i love drawing and colors and making things look pretty. art makes me feel calm and happy. sometimes it’s hard for me to use words, but pictures can show feelings too.
i think learning programs like photoshop and illustrator would be fun for me because i like being creative on the computer. i would like to design things for websites or social media that are bright and friendly. i might be shy, but i can put my ideas into pictures and designs. i would bring creativity, kindness, and imagination to the team.
submissions.assignment_name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company
The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body:
I’m really interested in both backend development and becoming a cybersecurity analyst because I enjoy working with computers in a deep, technical way — not just using programs, but understanding how systems actually work behind the scenes. I like solving problems, learning new skills, and thinking logically, and both of these careers challenge me and give me room to grow.

 

 

💻 Backend Development

 

 

In backend development, I’d be building the “brains” of applications — the systems that manage data, handle requests, and make sure everything works properly. It feels like solving puzzles with code and helping create tools that people rely on every day.

Typical salary range:

• Entry-level/backend developer: around $90,000–$125,000 per year on average in the U.S. depending on experience and company. 

• Mid-level/backend engineer roles can reach $125,000–$160,000+. 

• At top tech companies or in high-pay markets, backend engineers can earn above $150,000–$200,000 or more with bonuses/stock. 

 

 

🔐 Cybersecurity Analyst

 

 

In cybersecurity, I’d be protecting systems and data from threats — watching for attacks, hunting vulnerabilities, and defending people and companies from digital harm. That feels meaningful to me because it’s about keeping others safe, not just building things.

Typical salary range:

• Entry-level cybersecurity analyst roles often start around $70,000–$90,000 per year. 

• Average analysts in the U.S. make around $90,000–$120,000 per year. 

• Experienced cybersecurity professionals — especially with certifications and specialized skills — can earn $120,000–$170,000+ or more. 

 

 

One reason it’s hard for me to choose between the two is that both offer good salaries and growth potential — and both align with what I enjoy: technical problem-solving, continuous learning, and meaningful work. Backend development lets me create and build, while cybersecurity lets me protect and defend. Either path could support my family and give me a fulfilling long-term career.

 

So for me, the choice isn’t really about which one pays more right now — it’s about what kind of work I see myself loving every day: building systems or securing them. Both are strong careers with plenty of opportunities to grow and earn more as I gain experience.
submissions.assignment_name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body: I am drawn to AI/ML assisted research because of the multifaceted nature of the role. The prospect of contributing to the advancement of AI models, from enhancing their performance and logic to developing algorithms for ML pipelines, is particularly exciting. The opportunity to work with complex datasets, utilizing statistical methods for interpretation and creating insightful visualizations, aligns with my analytical inclinations. Furthermore, the potential to process clinical data and develop risk prediction models resonates with my interest in applying technology to real-world problems. While the median salary of $73,000 and the projected growth of 26% are encouraging, my primary motivation lies in the intellectual challenge and the chance to make a tangible impact. I am eager to learn more about specific research areas within this field and how my skills in data analysis and programming can best be applied. I envision myself contributing to this field by assisting in the design and execution of experiments, ensuring the accuracy of data analysis, and helping to translate complex findings into clear, actionable insights.
submissions.assignment_name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body: I am interested in looking into the roles that are less social, to be honest. I would like a job with a small community rather than a large community. I am interested in eventually getting into auto tech engineering. I am aware that it will take time and probably more schooling. I know someone who is in tech engineering at a factory, so I may look into that role. I also know someone who is in a role with AT&T, I believe they work with data analysis. I am interested in further investigating roles. I am aware that my interests may change over time.
submissions.assignment_name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body:
I can see myself in a role as a Product Manager.  I enjoy using research to determine the needs of a process or situation.  I also enjoy collaborating to build the framework of projects and watching them play out.  I think my investigations, logistics and even culinary arts will be beneficial in the level of attention to detail that is needed in this role.  I also have effective communication skills that will be very beneficial to a role as such.  

I think the Product Manager will also introduce me to other roles in tech that might spark my interest just in case I get bored or even if i wish to cross-train in depth.
submissions.assignment_name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body: I have chosen the role of executive assistant. The reason that is position stuck out to me is the fact the I get to work with the higher up positions but also the fact that the work is done with a small group of people and also I would be responsible to get thing ready for meetings, answer phone calls and directing the customers to the right person and department. Also the fact that I would be trusted with sensitive information and things that the executive would trust me. Also I am an organized person and I have strong communication skills. The way that I would be able to look up more information would be to look at potential companies, there website that has more information. Also just looking up any information that would help me to make my decision. Also because I am a helper so just being able to help the company and assets to the company.
submissions.assignment_name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body:
There is a spectrum of roles that peaked my interest in the Tech sphere of specialities.pdf


There is a spectrum of roles that peaked my interest in the Tech sphere of specialities. Some of them are entry level roles such as:

Content Creator/Editor, E-commerce Assistant,Helpdesk Technician,Web Content Manager & Social Media Specialist. 

As I have always had interest in the skills. That I do find tickles my fancy. I have already played around with for instance making my own websites, and being a content creator. I had always wanted to work in some way with Apple as this has been a long standing goal I've had since before AnnieCannons.

 

Then for the long term aspirations that would fit my interest are also a lengthy list which includes;

System Administrators/Network Administrator,Software Developer/Software Engineer,Backend Developer,DevOps Engineer,Data Scientist,UX/UI Designer,Data Analyst/Data Scientist

 

This excites me because I tend to like designing stuff that is “aesthetic”, and would love something with that.Along with the tendencies of mine that I had discovered in regards to analyzing data and trying to figure things out and find the trends or figure out the data points and how to interpret it. Along with my fascination with automation. 

 

I am also an ambivert/introvert so I would prefer a role that would align with such.

submissions.assignment_name: The Tech Community, Who Works at a Tech Company

submissions.body: I’m really interested in the Business Process Analysts roll. Recently I had my first job since 2013 in an office. I was completely confident everything would be a breeze until I started. I was overwhelmed. Instead of the company having a smooth system that flowed everything was unorganized and stressful. Everything was out of date, the sales were horrible, we had to write sticky notes for messages and get up and take it to the person across the office. I want to be able to identify what's wrong in a company and fix it. Coming up with a system that they can use to help boost their revenue and have operations running smoothly so their employees and customers are happy is my goal. I want to be the soul creator of or be able to better  the systems they are using.

submissions.body:
20260127_Assignment_ Career-Related Goal Worksheet (1).pdf

 

Career-Related Goal Worksheet

To help you get started, we've created a streamlined and easy-to-navigate worksheet designed just for you. This focused approach will make goal-setting clear, actionable, and achievable.

Step 1: Brainstorm Your Goals

A. Brainstorm Your Goals:

Write down work-related and personal goals without filtering.

  • Have my own apartment/housing (not with family; alone or with a partner).
  • Finish TCF Module successfully.
  • Gain custody of my son Taika and/or expanded rights regarding him.
  • Allowing myself to feel comfortable and gain confidence within myself and my skills in a meaningful and real way without falling to “imposter syndrome” type of traits.
  • Be more consistent along with being articulate & concise with a straightforward & coherent manner of communication.
  • Lose the weight gained from getting sober/being in rehab; in a healthy long term method.

B. Top 3–5 Work Goals

  • Completing TCF (aka the FIRST Module) 
  • Ability to fix all the issues with my personal project and get it to some basic functioning. (instead of very broken and redundant.)
  • Finishing the totality of AnnieCannons program successfully! (bonus if no pauses)
  • Obtaining an entry level position in the Tech field.
  • Become confident in my skills pertaining to coding/tech.

B. Annual and Quarterly Goals

  • Annual Goals: What do you need to accomplish this year?
  • Getting all my legal situations under control and handled. (Esp. family court and its pertaining to custody of Taika.)
  • Find stability concerning my income. (BONUS: if its multiple points of income revenues!)
  • “Locking in” when it comes to class and expanding my knowledge and ability in my tech skills.
  • Quarterly Goals: What do you need to accomplish this quarter?
  • Learning to co-parent in a healthy and consistent manner. (at least on my part; and being able to respond to any toxic behaviors in an efficient straight forward yet eloquent manner.)
  • Enhance my knowledge and comfortability in myself regarding a/any coding language.
  • Making an active effort in including myself in the community here at AnnieCannons.

C. Defining Success

  • What does success look like to you? (Number, feeling, etc.)

Confidence and having the ability to make decisions in my life that are mine and not based on someone else's decisions about my life. Having choices. Finding my peace. Perhaps finding myself documenting my life again through photos and videos and being able to share it wholeheartedly. (including my success; when appropriate.)

D. Immediate Steps

  • Identify 3–5 actionable steps to move towards your defined success.
  • Making more opportunities to push myself out of my comfort zone and be more of active part in life versus being an NPC.
  • Not allowing myself to fall behind or slack off. Keep on top of assignments, commitments and holding myself accountable even in times where it's uncomfortable or miniscule. 
  • Being okay with my mistakes and learning from myself. Allowing myself the ability to grow even more.

Step 2: Identify What You Want

  • List 10 things you want to achieve (personal, professional, etc.).

Step 3: Choose One Bold Career-Related Goal

A. Identify Your Vital Values- Values are things that really matter to you—qualities you already have (or aspire to have), the type of person you want to be. For instance, one person’s top values might be: To be optimistic, authentic, generous, loving, and spiritually plugged in.

Write your top values:

B. Define Your Goal

  1. I really want...(Choose a career-related goal from your list)
  • This goal is aligned with my values because...

  • My Bold Career-Related Goal:

 

  • Completion Date:

 

  1. Success Indicator: (I will know I have completed it when…)

Step 4: Prep for Obstacles

A. Anticipate Challenges

  • Your Turn to Brain Dump Your Obstacles! Write down potential obstacles:

B. Future Self Reflection

  • How would your future self who has achieved this goal approach challenges? Imagine yourself as the person who has already accomplished your goal. Reflect on how this future self would approach challenges.

Step 5: SMART Goal Breakdown

A. Specific:

  • Clearly define what you want to accomplish.

B. Measurable:

  • Determine how you will measure results.

C. Action-oriented and Aligned:

  • Outline specific actions and ensure alignment with aspirations.

D. Realistic and Relevant:

  • Ensure the goal is achievable and relevant.

E. Time-bound:

  • Set start and finish dates.

Step 6: Key Principles for Setting and Achieving Goals

A. Self-Belief:

  • Trust in your capability to achieve the goal.

B. Build a Supportive Community:

  • Identify your support network.

C. Prioritize Your Time:

  • Focus on activities that contribute to your goal.

D. Embrace and Adapt to Change:

  • Be open to feedback and adapt as needed.

E. Evaluate and Reflect:

  • Regularly assess your progress.

F. Celebrate Achievements:

  • Recognize and celebrate your successes.


Example of a student goal:

Key Principles for Setting and Achieving Goals
  • Self-Belief: Trust that you are capable.
  • Set Meaningful Goals: Ensure the internship goal matters to you.
  • Make SMART Goals: Specific, Measurable, Action-oriented, Realistic, Time-bound.
  • Build a Supportive Community: Engage with peers, mentors, and instructors.
  • Prioritize Your Time: Focus on activities that help you develop the skills needed for the internship.
  • Embrace and Adapt to Change: Be open to feedback and ready to adapt your approach.
  • Evaluate and Reflect: Regularly assess your progress.
  • Learn from Failure: Use setbacks as learning opportunities.
  • Focus on Progress: Celebrate small wins along the way.
  • Celebrate Achievements: Recognize and celebrate when you secure the internship.

 

SMART Goal Breakdown:
  • S - Specific: Secure an internship as a junior developer.
  • M - Measurable: Receive an internship offer and start working on real projects.
  • A - Action-oriented and Aligned: Apply for internships, prepare a portfolio, and practice interview skills.
  • R - Realistic and Relevant: Achievable with effort and relevant to career aspirations.
  • T - Time-bound: Complete this
